LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC.'S (DBA NIBBI BROTHER) MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT AND THEREAFTER, SEVER AND STAY ALL ACTIONS AGAINST CROSS DEFENDANTS IS DENIED. THE PROPOSED CROSS COMPLAINT SEEKS TO ADD A NEW PARTY AND THEORY TO THE LITIGATION ON THE EVE OF THE TRIAL WHICH WOULD PREJUDICE OTHER PARTIES AND IS NOT SUBSTANTIALLY JUSTIFIED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, CROSS DEFENDANT DOYLE'S WORK COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 4 AND 6 BECAUSE CROSS DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN. AT MOST IT COULD ESTABLISH THAT NIBBI WAS NEGLIGENT, BUT NOT THAT DOYLE AND/OR THE PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ALSO NEGLIGENT TO SOME DEGREE. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 THROUGH 3, GIVEN THE PARTIES' EXPRESS AGREEMENT AS TO THE SCOPE OF THE INDEMNITY OBLIGATION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: ATTY. SUIZI LIN OF FURTADO JASPOVICE & SIMONS, TEL. 510-582-8254, FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEVEN M. MARDEN OF DELA HOUSAYE AND ASSOCIATES, TEL. 925-944-3300, FOR DEFENDANT NIBBI BROS ASSOCIATES;
ATTY. MONICA WILEY OF CARLSON CALLADINE & PETERSON LLP, TEL 391-9851, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY DOYLE'S WORK COMPANY.

RULING: A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT DOYLE'S WORK COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 4 AND 6 BECAUSE CROSS DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN. AT MOST IT COULD ESTABLISH THAT NIBBI WAS NEGLIGENT, BUT NOT THAT DOYLE AND/OR THE PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ALSO NEGLIGENT TO SOME DEGREE. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 THROUGH 3, GIVEN THE PARTIES' EXPRESS AGREEMENT AS TO THE SCOPE OF THE INDEMNITY OBLIGATION. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =(301/PJB/PM)
